items.title: UPDATE: Ammar Awawdeh — the co-conspirator PTFO reported on in Part I of our NBA gambling investigation, across the cases of Malik Beasley, Jontay Porter, and Terry Rozier — has just reached a plea agreement with the Department of Justice. 🚨🫎

items.title: [ESPN] Terry Rozier’s bid to modify his bail conditions and try returning to the NBA has been denied. Rozier violated the no-contact provision of his $3 million bond by texting someone on the no-contact list to tell them they were on the no-contact list
items.author: The_MadStork
items.body_excerpt: https://www.espn.com/nba/story/\_/id/49310839/judge-denies-terry-rozier-request-modify-bail-conditions Former Miami Heat guard Terry Rozier wants to play in the NBA while he fights sports gambling charges that sidelined him last season. Bail conditions that bar him from contact…

items.title: [Vorkunov] Terry Rozier continues to seek a job with an NBA team. His current bond terms would prevent that because of how the NBA views them, his lawyer said, with ex-teammates now on 4 different teams. He's unable to play against or practice with them, per his bond terms. Judge denied the request.
items.author: PlayaSlayaX
items.body_excerpt: Context: Terry Rozier was indicted, alongside Chauncey Billups and Damon Jones, on illegal gambling charges. Rozier, in particular, is accused with manipulating prop bets on himself in at least seven games he played in between February 2023–March 2024.

items.title: Rozier contacted one person on the government's no contact list. His lawyer said it was just to let the person know he couldn't talk to them because they were on that list.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Rozier contacted one person on the government's no contact list. His lawyer said it was just to let the person know he couldn't talk to them because they were on that list. 

"You violated the court's trust when you sent this text message," federal judge LaShann DeArcy Hall said.

items.title: "I wish we were starting trial on this case because Terry is innocent and we look forward to showing the world that he had no involvement in this," David Markus, Terry Rozier's new lawyer, said after the hearing.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: "I wish we were starting trial on this case because Terry is innocent and we look forward to showing the world that he had no involvement in this," David Markus, Terry Rozier's new lawyer, said after the hearing.

items.title: Spicy day in Brooklyn federal court as Terry Rozier made his latest appearance. A federal judge denied his request to remove certain ex-Hornets players + coaches from no contact list. Rozier's new lawyer has asked judge to move the case to South Florida. Trial date still Feb. 8.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Spicy day in Brooklyn federal court as Terry Rozier made his latest appearance. A federal judge denied his request to remove certain ex-Hornets players + coaches from no contact list. Rozier's new lawyer has asked judge to move the case to South Florida. Trial date still Feb. 8.

items.title: NBA told EDNY prosecutors they prefer Terry Rozier keeps bond conditions that prevent him from playing in NBA.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
NBA told EDNY prosecutors they prefer Terry Rozier keeps bond conditions that prevent him from playing in NBA. 

Feds say Rozier texted with someone on his no-contact list and are investigating if co-defendant contacted witnesses about the case, possibly on his behalf www.nytimes.com/athletic/736...

media.title: NBA, Hornets ask prosecutors to keep Terry Rozier’s no-contact order in place
media.description: Federal prosecutors said that Rozier has already texted one person on the list of people he is not allowed to contact.

items.title: Wrote about the big NBA thing that happened today: the latest hearing for Terry Rozier in federal court and a request from a federal judge for the NBA to advise whether they want him to be able to play in the NBA.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt:
Wrote about the big NBA thing that happened today: the latest hearing for Terry Rozier in federal court and a request from a federal judge for the NBA to advise whether they want him to be able to play in the NBA.

Free to read for all: www.nytimes.com/athletic/734...

media.title: Terry Rozier’s trial date set for 2027 as NBA future grows uncertain
media.description: Rozier has pleaded not guilty to all four charges. The judge set a Feb. 8, 2027 date for trial.

items.title: Terry Rozier pleads not guilty to new charges; Feb. trial set
items.author: ESPN
items.body_excerpt: Terry Rozier pleads not guilty to new charges; Feb. trial set ESPN

items.title: Rozier's lawyer has asked the judge to lift a bond restriction that effectively keeps him from playing in the NBA. Judge has asked the NBA to weigh in by Monday. The trial date for the case has been set for Feb. 8, 2027. Rozier has asked the judge to dismiss the charges.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Rozier's lawyer has asked the judge to lift a bond restriction that effectively keeps him from playing in the NBA. Judge has asked the NBA to weigh in by Monday. The trial date for the case has been set for Feb. 8, 2027. Rozier has asked the judge to dismiss the charges.

items.title: Jim Trusty, lawyer for Terry Rozier: "The NBA has literally flagrantly fouled the presumption of innocence...They're still trying to find ways to pretend they're victims & that Terry should be prohibited from playing basketball... They are not on the side of angels in this case."
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
items.body_excerpt: Jim Trusty, lawyer for Terry Rozier: "The NBA has literally flagrantly fouled the presumption of innocence...They're still trying to find ways to pretend they're victims & that Terry should be prohibited from playing basketball... They are not on the side of angels in this case."

items.title: The trial for Terry Rozier and the 3 other defendants in the NBA insider trading gambling case has been set to begin Feb. 8, 2027.
items.author: Mike Vorkunov
